inline.body:
<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Removal of copyDir violates .review-rules/copy.md — The PR deletes the exported `copyDir` function from `utils.ts`. The project rule in `.review-rules/copy.md` explicitly states: 'Recursive / directory file copies MUST go through the project's copyDir helper in packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts. Do NOT call fs.cpSync / fs.cp directly … A change that removes copyDir or swaps it for fs.cpSync must be flagged.' This is a trusted, precedence-taking rule.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — copyDir replaced with fs.cpSync, violating .review-rules/copy.md — The PR swaps `copyDir(publicDir, outDir)` for `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, … })` in `prepareOutDir.ts`. This directly violates the project rule that forbids replacing `copyDir` with `fs.cpSync`. Even ignoring the rule, the swap is not behavior-preserving (see related findings).

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Lost srcFile === destDir guard — behavior change for nested outDir — The old `copyDir` implementation skipped the destination directory itself when `srcFile === destDir`, handling the case where `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir` (e.g., user runs with `public/` containing a `dist/` entry). This guard is not reproduced by the new `fs.cpSync` call. Node's `fs.cpSync` will throw `ERR_FS_CP_COPY_TO_SUBDIRECTORY` in some configurations instead of skipping gracefully, making this an observable behavioral change for real project layouts.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — fs.cpSync has different symlink and error semantics across Node versions — The old implementation used `fs.statSync` (follows symlinks) and `fs.copyFileSync` per leaf, giving a deterministic and simple error surface. `fs.cpSync` carries many defaults (`force`, `errorOnExist`, `preserveTimestamps`, `followSymlinks`, `mode`) whose behavior has shifted across Node versions. The eslint-disable comment concedes the API surface isn't stable across supported Node versions. The `mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE` is a no-op on most filesystems and should be justified. The `n/no-unsupported-features/node-builtins` suppression needs a concrete Node engine-floor argument.

<!-- squarewright:inline -->

**Correctness, Security** — Same copyDir-to-fs.cpSync swap in create-vite with lost behavioral equivalence — The `create-vite` package's local `copy`/`copyDir` pair is deleted and replaced with `fs.cpSync(path.join(templateDir, file), targetPath, { recursive: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })`. This has the same class of regression as the vite package: a hand-rolled directory copy swapped for the stdlib call without establishing that the stdlib defaults match the old behavior across the supported Node range and platforms. The `srcFile === destDir` guard was not present here, so that specific issue is vite-only, but the broader 'fs.cpSync has different defaults' concern applies to both.

sticky:
<!-- squarewright:review -->

## Squarewright review

The PR directly violates the project's trusted precedence-taking rule in .review-rules/copy.md by deleting copyDir and replacing it with fs.cpSync — the two actions the rule explicitly forbids. Independent of the rule, the swap loses a srcFile===destDir guard (behavioral change for nested outDir) and introduces unstated assumptions about fs.cpSync defaults across the supported Node range. Recommend reverting to copyDir and opening a focused discussion (ADR) before changing this convention.

**5** finding(s): 🔴 3  ·  🟡 2  ·  🔵 0

- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts:624` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Removal of copyDir violates .review-rules/copy.md — The PR deletes the exported `copyDir` function from `utils.ts`. The project rule in `.review-rules/copy.md` explicitly states: 'Recursive / directory file copies MUST go through the project's copyDir helper in packages/vite/src/node/utils.ts. Do NOT call fs.cpSync / fs.cp directly … A change that removes copyDir or swaps it for fs.cpSync must be flagged.' This is a trusted, precedence-taking rule.
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — copyDir replaced with fs.cpSync, violating .review-rules/copy.md — The PR swaps `copyDir(publicDir, outDir)` for `fs.cpSync(publicDir, outDir, { recursive: true, … })` in `prepareOutDir.ts`. This directly violates the project rule that forbids replacing `copyDir` with `fs.cpSync`. Even ignoring the rule, the swap is not behavior-preserving (see related findings).
- 🔴 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Lost srcFile === destDir guard — behavior change for nested outDir — The old `copyDir` implementation skipped the destination directory itself when `srcFile === destDir`, handling the case where `outDir` is nested inside `publicDir` (e.g., user runs with `public/` containing a `dist/` entry). This guard is not reproduced by the new `fs.cpSync` call. Node's `fs.cpSync` will throw `ERR_FS_CP_COPY_TO_SUBDIRECTORY` in some configurations instead of skipping gracefully, making this an observable behavioral change for real project layouts.
- 🟡 `packages/vite/src/node/plugins/prepareOutDir.ts:89` _[Correctness, Security]_ — fs.cpSync has different symlink and error semantics across Node versions — The old implementation used `fs.statSync` (follows symlinks) and `fs.copyFileSync` per leaf, giving a deterministic and simple error surface. `fs.cpSync` carries many defaults (`force`, `errorOnExist`, `preserveTimestamps`, `followSymlinks`, `mode`) whose behavior has shifted across Node versions. The eslint-disable comment concedes the API surface isn't stable across supported Node versions. The `mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE` is a no-op on most filesystems and should be justified. The `n/no-unsupported-features/node-builtins` suppression needs a concrete Node engine-floor argument.
- 🟡 `packages/create-vite/src/index.ts:647` _[Correctness, Security]_ — Same copyDir-to-fs.cpSync swap in create-vite with lost behavioral equivalence — The `create-vite` package's local `copy`/`copyDir` pair is deleted and replaced with `fs.cpSync(path.join(templateDir, file), targetPath, { recursive: true, mode: fs.constants.COPYFILE_FICLONE })`. This has the same class of regression as the vite package: a hand-rolled directory copy swapped for the stdlib call without establishing that the stdlib defaults match the old behavior across the supported Node range and platforms. The `srcFile === destDir` guard was not present here, so that specific issue is vite-only, but the broader 'fs.cpSync has different defaults' concern applies to both.
